metadata.summary: Rebinning of powder diffraction data taken with multiple analyzer crystals and a large area detector

documentation:
[![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/DOI/10.5281/zenodo.7223895.svg)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.7223895)

# Multianalyzer
Rebinning of powder diffraction data taken with multiple analyzer crystals and a large area detector

This project is about the rebinning of data comming from the [high resolution powder diffraction beamline (ID22) at the ESRF](https://www.esrf.fr/id22). 
It implements the math which are described in [J. Appl. Cryst. (2021). 54, 1088-1099](https://doi.org/10.1107/S1600576721005288).

There are two implementations, Cython parallelized with OpenMP and OpenCL running on GPU.
The later is faster. 

The main executable is `id22rebin` which takes a BLISS-HDF5 file with ROI-collections in it (as generated on the ID22 beamline) and rebins the intensities 
of the diffeent ROI, after calculating their precise position.
The geometry is described in an output file from TOPAS.
Intensities are grouped per analyzer crystal and per column (depth dependent) and need to be normalized.   

## Installation
`pip install multianalyzer`

## Development version:
```
git clone https://github.com/kif/multianalyzer
cd multianalyzer
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install .
```

The tests are only validating input file reading.

classes.name: multianalyzer.utils.ProgressBar

methods.name: multianalyzer.utils.ProgressBar.__init__
documentation:
Create a progress bar using a title, a maximum value and a graphical size.

The display is done with stdout using carriage return to to hide the
previous progress. It is not possible to use stdout for something else
whill a progress bar is in use.

The result looks like:

```none
Title [■■■■■■      ]  50%  Message
```

```
param str title

Title displayed before the progress bar

param float max_value

The maximum value of the progress bar

param int bar_width

Size of the progressbar in the screen
```

methods.name: multianalyzer.utils.ProgressBar.clear
documentation:
Remove the progress bar from the display and move the cursor
at the beginning of the line using carriage return.

methods.name: multianalyzer.utils.ProgressBar.display
documentation: Display the progress bar to stdout

methods.name: multianalyzer.utils.ProgressBar.update
documentation:
Update the progrss bar with the progress bar's current value.

Set the progress bar's current value, compute the percentage
of progress and update the screen with. Carriage return is used
first and then the content of the progress bar. The cursor is
at the begining of the line.

:param float value: progress bar's current value
:param str message: message displayed after the progress bar
:param float max_value: If not none, update the maximum value of the
    progress bar
Progress bar in shell mode

methods.name: multianalyzer.opencl.OclMultiAnalyzer.__init__
documentation:
Constructor if the "Multi-analyzer" working on OpenCL

Nota:
Internally, all calculation are performed in radians.
All distances must use the same unit (m, mm or inches)

```
param L

distance from the sample to the analyzer

param L2

distance from the analyzer to the detector

param pixel

pixel size

param center

position of the center on the detector (in pixel)

param tha

acceptance angle of the analyzer crystal(°)

param thd

diffraction angle of the analyzer crystal(°) 2x tha

param psi

Offset of angles (in 2th) of the analyzer crystals

param rollx

mis-orientation of the analyzer along x (°)

param rolly

mis-orientation of the analyzer along y (°)

param device

2-tuple with the device
```

methods.name: multianalyzer.opencl.OclMultiAnalyzer.integrate
documentation:
Performess the integration of the ROIstack recorded at given angles on t

```
param roi_stack

stack of (nframes,NUM_CRYSTAL*numROI) with the recorded signal

param arm

2theta position of the arm (in degrees)

param tth_min

start position of the histograms (in degrees)

param tth_max

End positon of the histogram (in degrees)

param dtth

bin size for the histogram (in degrees)

param phi_max

discard data with phi larger than this value (in degree)

param iter_max

maximum number of iteration in the 2theta convergence

param resolution

precision of the 2theta convergence in fraction of dtth

param width

width of the sample, same unit as pixels

param dtthw

Minimum precision expected for ROI being  appart, by default dtth

return

center of bins, histogram of signal and histogram of normalization, cycles per data-point
```

methods.name: multianalyzer.opencl.OclMultiAnalyzer.init_integrate
documentation:
Initializes the integrator for the rebinning of several small chunks of data.

```
param max_frames

number of frames (max) per block

param arm

2theta position of the arm (in degrees)

param tth_min

start position of the histograms (in degrees)

param tth_max

End positon of the histogram (in degrees)

param dtth

bin size for the histogram (in degrees)

param phi_max

discard data with phi larger than this value (in degree)

param iter_max

maximum number of iteration in the 2theta convergence

param resolution

precision of the 2theta convergence in fraction of dtth

param width

width of the sample, same unit as pixels

param dtthw

Minimum precision expected for ROI being  appart, by default dtth

return

nothing.
```
Multi pass implementation

functions.name: multianalyzer.file_io.topas_parser
documentation:
Parser for TOPAS output file with geometry of the multianalyzer

```
param infile

name of the file containing the TOPAS refined parameters, usually 'out7.pars'

return

dict with the data from the file (no conversion is made)
```

functions.name: multianalyzer.file_io.ID22_bliss_parser
documentation:
Read an HDF5 file as generated by BLISS on ID22 and returns valuable information from there

```
param infile

name of the HDF5 file

param entries

list/set of entries to be processed

param exclude_entries

list/set of entries excluded

param block_size

chunk the RoiCollection into blocks not larger than this size (default TOTAL_MEM/4)

return

dict with entries containing each:
* tha: the acceptance angle of the analyzer
* thd: the diffractio angle of the analyzer (~2*tha)
* roicol: A collection of ROI for each frame OR
* roicol_lst: a list of BlockDescriptor to be read by the RoiColReader
* mon: a monitor for the intensity of the incoming beam
* arm: the position of the 2theta arm when collecting
```

documentation:
Return the attribute of the dataset

```

a HDF5 dataset (or a group)

name of the attribute

default value to be returned

return

attribute value decoded in python3 or default
```

Writer class to handle Nexus/HDF5 data

Manages:

- entry

    - pyFAI-subentry

        - detector
